description:
You are playing the role of an in-house penetration tester who has just pulled a Nessus-style CSV export of hosts and vulnerabilities into the workstation.  
 Your manager only wants to see HIGH and CRITICAL findings in a very compact, tab-separated file that can be pasted directly into an internal wiki.  
 All work must be done from the Linux terminal with classic text-processing utilities such as **cut**, **paste**, **grep**, **sort**, **uniq**, **tee**, etc.

 Initial data location (already present when you start the task):
 • Directory: /home/user/scan_results  
 • File: /home/user/scan_results/raw_scan.csv

  ‑ The file is comma-separated and includes one header row followed by vulnerability rows.  
  ‑ Columns (in order) are:
    1. target_ip
    2. hostname
    3. port
    4. service
    5. cve_id
    6. cvss_score
    7. severity
    8. description

 What you must deliver
 1. Create a **new file** /home/user/scan_results/high_severity_report.tsv that satisfies **all** of the following, byte-for-byte:
    a. It must be UTF-8 plain text, using **tab characters** (ASCII 0x09) as field separators — no spaces.  
    b. The very first line is a header exactly (case-sensitive)  
       IP<TAB>CVE_ID<TAB>SEVERITY<TAB>PORT  
    c. Include **only** rows where the original “severity” column is either HIGH or CRITICAL (anything else is excluded).  
    d. Each output row must contain the fields, in this order:  
       • target_ip  
       • cve_id  
       • severity (unchanged, still upper-case)  
       • port  
    e. Sort rows using the following multi-key rule:  
       • primary key: target_ip (ascending, dotted-quad textual sort)  
       • secondary key: cve_id (ascending, ASCII sort)  
       • tertiary key: port (numerical ascending)  
    f. The file must end with a single newline (UNIX LF) and must **not** contain any trailing blank lines or extra whitespace.

 2. Create a log file /home/user/scan_results/report_generation.log that contains exactly three lines, each terminated with a single LF:
    Line 1: START YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS  
    Line 2: CREATED high_severity_report.tsv LINES=N  
            – Replace N with the total number of data rows written **excluding** the header.  
    Line 3: END YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS  
    • The timestamps on lines 1 and 3 must be identical to the moment each line is written (second precision; any valid current timestamp is acceptable).  
    • Use 24-hour clock and zero-padding (e.g., 2024-05-09 14:07:03).  
    • Use spaces, not tabs, inside the log.  
    • The file must contain no other text.

 Verification details the grader will check:
 • /home/user/scan_results/high_severity_report.tsv exists, is readable, contains exactly 8 lines (1 header + 7 data lines) and matches the format/rules above.  
 • Each data line has exactly four tab-separated fields and no embedded commas or spaces.  
 • Rows are correctly filtered (only HIGH or CRITICAL severities) and correctly sorted by the stated keys.  
 • /home/user/scan_results/report_generation.log exists, has exactly three LF-terminated lines, follows the specified token layout, contains the correct data-line count, and uses valid timestamps.

 Hints (not exhaustive):
 • `grep -E '(HIGH|CRITICAL)'` can help isolate severities.  
 • Because you must sort on columns that are not all present in the final output (e.g., port), you may find it easier to keep temporary working files or use `paste` to re-attach columns for sorting before cutting them away.  
 • Remember to convert commas to tabs only after you have selected the lines you need, otherwise field positions will shift.  
 • Avoid superfluous whitespace; the test harness performs a byte-for-byte comparison.

 The task is complete when both target files exist in the correct location and all format requirements are met.
truth:
Existing file before the agent starts:
 • Path: /home/user/scan_results/raw_scan.csv  
 • Content (8 columns, comma-separated, LF line endings):

target_ip,hostname,port,service,cve_id,cvss_score,severity,description
192.168.1.10,webserver1,80,http,CVE-2021-1234,7.5,HIGH,Some description
192.168.1.11,db1,3306,mysql,CVE-2020-9999,5.3,MEDIUM,Some description
192.168.1.12,ftp1,21,ftp,CVE-2019-2222,9.8,CRITICAL,Some description
192.168.1.10,webserver1,443,https,CVE-2021-5678,4.3,LOW,Some description
192.168.1.13,app1,8080,http,CVE-2022-1111,8.0,HIGH,Some description
192.168.1.12,ftp1,21,ftp,CVE-2019-3333,6.1,MEDIUM,Some description
192.168.1.14,mail,25,smtp,CVE-2021-0001,10.0,CRITICAL,Some description
192.168.1.10,webserver1,80,http,CVE-2021-7777,9.0,CRITICAL,Some description
192.168.1.13,app1,8080,http,CVE-2022-2222,9.5,CRITICAL,Some description
192.168.1.11,db1,3306,mysql,CVE-2020-8888,7.0,HIGH,Some description

 Expected final artefacts after a correct solution:

1. /home/user/scan_results/high_severity_report.tsv  
   Exact byte content (LF line endings, TABs between fields):  
IP	CVE_ID	SEVERITY	PORT
192.168.1.10	CVE-2021-1234	HIGH	80
192.168.1.10	CVE-2021-7777	CRITICAL	80
192.168.1.11	CVE-2020-8888	HIGH	3306
192.168.1.12	CVE-2019-2222	CRITICAL	21
192.168.1.13	CVE-2022-1111	HIGH	8080
192.168.1.13	CVE-2022-2222	CRITICAL	8080
192.168.1.14	CVE-2021-0001	CRITICAL	25

2. /home/user/scan_results/report_generation.log  
   – Three lines, LF endings.  
   – Because the timestamps will depend on runtime, the grader will only check:  
     • Line 1 starts with "START " and has exactly 19 characters after it (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS).  
     • Line 2 is "CREATED high_severity_report.tsv LINES=7" (nothing more, nothing less).  
     • Line 3 starts with "END " and has exactly 19 characters after it (same timestamp format).  
   Example acceptable content:  
START 2024-05-09 14:07:01
CREATED high_severity_report.tsv LINES=7
END 2024-05-09 14:07:03
name: task_000000_e017019b
